desc: Half of the requests omit a timeout at random, allowing the process to hang indefinitely on a stalled connection.
The public kind argument is concatenated directly into the URL without encoding, so reserved characters can alter or corrupt the query string.
Random request counts, parameters, and timeout behavior make identical calls produce unpredictable results and failures, preventing reliable testing and operation.
parse_response returns a dictionary or string depending on the outcome, forcing callers to handle an undocumented heterogeneous result type.
main catches every operational exception, discards all accumulated work, and exits successfully, preventing callers and monitoring systems from detecting failure.
The module-level Session is only closed by main, so importing and using these functions as a library can leave its connection resources open.
parse_response catches every exception from JSON processing and misreports programming or runtime failures as merely non-JSON content.
